reasoning:
The NFL owners tabled a ban on the "Tush Push" at the March/April 2025 Annual League Meeting due to a 16-16 split in an informal vote, well short of the 24 votes needed. However, the proposal was deferred to the Spring League Meeting scheduled for May 20-21, 2025 (safely before the June 1 deadline). Recent reporting from early May 2025 by prominent insiders (e.g., Mark Maske of The Washington Post) indicates that a revised, "expansive" proposal will be voted on in May. Crucially, the new proposal broadens the ban to prohibit any teammate from pushing or pulling the ball carrier anywhere on the field, rather than just targeting the quarterback sneak. 

This strategic expansion likely serves as a compromise to win over owners who were previously hesitant to pass a rule that seemed to specifically target one team (the Philadelphia Eagles) or a single type of play. Maske reports the new proposal is "expected to have a reasonably good chance of being approved." While flipping 8 votes is typically challenging, the fact that the competition committee is bringing it back to the floor in May—combined with insider confidence—suggests that the league office has likely whipped enough votes using the broadened language. I estimate a 71% chance that the proposal secures the required supermajority at the May meeting.
